articles.title: Trump makes big announcement as many wonder about POTUS' whereabouts: ‘I’ll be going...'
articles.description: President Donald Trump made a major announcement about his future plans on Truth Social as many wondered why he had not been seen in public for about a week.

articles.title: Jaishankar highlights deep civilisational bonds with Laos at JCM
articles.description: EAM S Jaishankar and Laos DPM Thongsavanh Phomvihane co-chaired the 10th Joint Commission Meeting in New Delhi, highlighting deep civilisational ties and reviewing strategic pacts as the nations mark 70 years of diplomatic relations.

articles.title: High Drama! Rubio Humiliated To His Face With Back-To-Back 'Sleeping' Videos Of Trump
articles.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio found himself under intense scrutiny during a House hearing after Congressman Ted Lieu played videos that he argued showed U.S. President Donald Trump asleep during public events. The exchange quickly escalated into a broader debate over Trump's health, cognitive tests, and recent absence from public appearances.

articles.title: Iran targets US military vessel's 'command center' in Gulf of Oman, Centcom dismisses claim
articles.description: Iran's state media reported its navy targeted a US destroyer in the Gulf of Oman, citing US actions against Iranian commercial vessels. The US Central Command refuted the claim, stating its military assets at sea are operating safely and unimpeded.

articles.title: 19,000 of 1.2 lakh granted citizenship under CAA: CM
articles.description: Kolkata: Of the 1.2 lakh Citizenship Amendment Act (CAA) applicants in Bengal, 19,000 have already been granted Indian citizenship certificates, CM Su.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Aryna Sabalenka: “Sometimes you just need to let it go, to empty it so you’re ready to start over”
articles.description: World number 1 Aryna Sabalenka might have faced a brutal and a shocking loss in the quarterfinals of the French Open on June 3 against Diana Shnaider but she has constantly proven how tough she is even though she has been pretty emotional about it. In the last few hours, the talented player Aryna Sabalenka has expressed her disappointment and frustration as she has even spoken about quitting tennis. While she was spotted screaming on court as she lost the game, she recently spoke about being vocal about her emotions.

articles.title: Albanian protestsers demonstrate over Trump family-linked resort
articles.description: TIRANA, Albania — A massive coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.S. President Donald Trump, is facing growing resistance from protesters in Albania. The government says the development on the Adriatic coast would be transformational for the former communist nation as it seeks to enter the high-end ...

articles.title: Bibi Using Trump-Epstein Videos To Arm-Twist? ‘If You Force Israel...’: Shocking Spat | TOI Explain
articles.description: The question of whether Benjamin Netanyahu is blackmailing Donald Trump with alleged Epstein-related videos has gone viral online, fuelled by growing political tension and recent clashes between the two leaders. While some social media narratives link their strained relationship to hidden leverage, there is no verified evidence supporting any claim of blackmail. What is clear, however, is a widening rift over Middle East strategy, especially regarding Israel’s actions in Lebanon and broader regional pressure involving Iran, which has intensified speculation and debate worldwide.

articles.title: ‘Unauthorised’ school in Chunabhatti ordered shut, students told to relocate
articles.description: Claiming that the school has misguided them, parents complained that the school charged them full fees for the new academic year, stating that there is no reason to worry.

articles.title: Public to be part of 12 yrs of Modi govt celebrations: CM
articles.description: UP CM Yogi Adityanath reviews law and order, June 8-10 police constable exam for 29 lakh candidates, and June 5-21 public campaigns incl 5 crore saplings.

articles.title: Vijay Gifts Lone Rajya Sabha Seat To Congress, Signals Alliance Unity
articles.description: Announcing the decision, Vijay said the seat would be allotted to Congress as part of the TVK-led alliance in the state.

articles.title: India gets fourth S-400 'Sudarshan' air defence system from Russia
articles.description: India's long-range air defence gets a major boost with the arrival of the fourth S-400 'Sudarshan' air defence system squadron from Russia. The system will be deployed in its operational area very soon, as per defence sources.

articles.title: Mamata's ruthlessness has reduced TMC to 'pieces', claims KP Maurya
articles.description: UP Deputy CM Keshav Prasad Maurya claimed Mamata Banerjee's "ruthlessness" has disintegrated the TMC. Dilip Ghosh echoed this, linking the party's internal crisis to its "family rule" and predicting its eventual collapse amid a rebel challenge.

articles.title: Congress central body to pick candidate for TN Rajya Sabha seat: Chodankar
articles.description: After TVK allotted a Rajya Sabha seat to its ally Congress in Tamil Nadu, party leader Girish Chodankar stated the candidate will be finalised by the central election authority. The election for the seat is scheduled to be held on June 18.

articles.title: Vance or Rubio? Trump drops his biggest clue yet about who could lead Republicans after him
articles.description: US President Donald Trump said Vice President JD Vance and Secretary of State Marco Rubio would be an unbeatable Republican ticket in 2028, praising their relationship and political appeal.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Oscar Wilde: 'When a man has once loved a woman he will do anything for her except continue to love her...' The Irish writer's timeless lesson on how love and relationships change with time
articles.description: Oscar Wilde’s quote of the day offers a witty yet thought-provoking reflection on how romantic feelings can change over time. The celebrated Irish writer, known for his sharp observations on human nature, suggests that while affection, care, and loyalty may remain, lasting romantic love is not always permanent. The quote highlights the complexities and contradictions of relationships, a theme that appears throughout Wilde’s work.

articles.title: Congress MP from TN boycotts Karnataka CM swearing-in ceremony for his statement on Mekedatu dam issue
articles.description: Congress MP from TN boycotts Karnataka CM swearing-in ceremony for his statement on Mekedatu dam issue

articles.title: National Lok Adalat on June 13
articles.description: Join the National Lok Adalat in Tiruchi on June 13 for settling pending legal cases efficiently.

articles.title: Journalist, IIT-ian among spokespersons named by Cockroach Party ahead of Saturday’s protest
articles.description: The party said they will represent the movement before the public and media as it seeks to "change the political discourse of India" through a new generation of leaders.

articles.title: "I Was A Little Perturbed": Trump Admits Calling Netanyahu "F***ing Crazy"
articles.description: The exchange underscored the shaky ties between two right-wing allies who started the war

articles.title: Punjab to cap private school fee hikes at 5% following Amritsar girl’s death
articles.description: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann said a stringent ordinance against arbitrary fee hikes by private schools in the state will be brought in the next one or two days.

articles.title: Maharashtra-style split fears rise as TMC rebels cross crucial strength mark
articles.description: The internal crisis in the Trinamool Congress has intensified after a rebel faction claimed the support of 58 MLAs in the West Bengal Assembly.

articles.title: Amid rebellion, TMC dissolves all committees in Bengal, announces organisational overhaul
articles.description: TMC crisis: Trinamool Congress dissolved all organisational committees in West Bengal and announced a review of its structure amid rebellion by MLAs. The move comes as party leaders seek to rebuild and address internal challenges.

articles.title: Maharashtra council polls | Mahayuti urges rebel candidates to withdraw from race; MVA faces withdrawals
articles.description: Mahayuti leaders urged rebel candidates to withdraw before Maharashtra Legislative Council polls, aiming to secure all 17 seats. The opposition MVA faces setbacks after nominee withdrawals and disqualifications, ahead of June 18 voting.

articles.title: Prolonged matrimonial litigation only leads to perpetuity of marriage on paper: Supreme Court
articles.description: Supreme Court Divorce: Discover how prolonged matrimonial cases only sustain marriage on paper as the Supreme Court dissolves a 15-year-old union.

articles.title: From supporters to critics: Why Nepal's students are protesting against PM Balen Shah
articles.description: A political storm has erupted in Nepal after PM Balen Shah's remarks on Kalapani, Lipulekh and Limpiyadhura triggered protests, opposition backlash and calls for his resignation.

articles.title: Cockroach Janta Party appoints journalist, IIT graduate, filmmaker as official spokespersons ahead of June 6 Delhi protest; who are they?
articles.description: It should be noted that the party launched two weeks ago which quickly took social media by storm, gaining millions of followers in a couple of days.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee insulted B R Ambedkar by paying tributes to him while wearing slippers: BJP's Shehzad Poonawalla
articles.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has accused former West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee of disrespecting B R Ambedkar. A video purportedly shows her paying floral tributes to the Dalit icon while wearing footwear. The BJP claims this highlights the Trinamool Congress's alleged anti-Ambedkar stance and vote-bank politics. BJP national spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla shared a short video clip on X, and said "insulting" Ambedkar has become the identity of the Trinamool Congress.

articles.title: D. K. Shivakumar invites Siddaramaiah, B.S. Yediyurappa, H.D. Deve Gowda to swearing-in ceremony in Bengaluru
articles.description: The visits were seen as an attempt to acknowledge their contribution to Karnataka’s political landscape.

articles.title: Mamata Banerjee-Led TMC Dissolves All Committees, Frontal Organisations In West Bengal
articles.description: TMC dissolves all Bengal units as expelled MLA Ritabrata Banerjee claims support of 60 legislators, deepening the party's biggest internal crisis in years.

articles.title: Putin's Russian Davos opens under war's shadow
articles.description: Russia hosts its premier investment forum in Saint Petersburg. The event includes prominent figures from the United States and Germany. This gathering occurs as Russia faces economic stagnation and ongoing conflict in Ukraine. The forum aims for pragmatic dialogue on a stable future. Discussions will cover energy, artificial intelligence, and investment.

articles.title: CBSE Student Sarthak Sidhant Flags OSM Tender Irregularities, Board Says Will Respond In Writing
articles.description: Sarthak Sidhant, an 18-year-old CBSE student from Jharkhand who was directly affected by the system, made a detailed presentation before the committee.

articles.title: 'F*ck India! F*ck India': Texas Man Tears Indian Flag In Front Of Cheering Crowd, Viral Video Sparks Outrage
articles.description: A viral video showing a Texas man tearing an Indian flag outside Frisco City Hall has sparked outrage and debate online

articles.title: Kerala Deputy Opposition Leader issue will be resolved through talks within LDF, says Binoy Viswam
articles.description: Binoy Viswam assures resolution of Deputy Opposition Leader issue in Kerala through dialogue within the Left Democratic Front (LDF).

articles.title: UK police review race plan amid anger over treatment of student stabbed by Indian-origin man
articles.description: British police chiefs are reviewing anti-racism guidance following outrage over the treatment of a white student, who was handcuffed as he lay dying after being stabbed by a Sikh man, officials said Wednesday. Policing minister Sarah Jones said it was "right" that the guidance was being reviewed.

articles.title: 1st batch of ministers to take oath with Shivakumar
articles.description: DK Shivakumar will take oath as Chief Minister of Karnataka today. A first batch of ministers will be sworn in with him. A second phase of cabinet expansion will occur after June 18, following Rajya Sabha elections. Seniority will be considered along with other factors for portfolio allocation. The final list of ministers will be released today.

articles.title: Who Is Bill Pulte, Known As Trump's "Attack Dog" Named US New Intel Chief
articles.description: Bill Pulte is the chief of the Federal Housing Finance Agency.

articles.title: 'He was heavily into astrology': Software developer builds website for Rs 3,000, calls it worst freelance job ever
articles.description: A developer shared that his Rs 3,000 freelance website project turned into a stressful experience due to long, off-topic daily meetings, unusual personal conversations, and repeated distractions from the actual work.

articles.title: Who is Khalilur Rahman, new UNGA chief?
articles.description: Bangladesh's Foreign Minister, Dr. Khalilur Rahman, will lead the United Nations General Assembly. He secured the presidency for the 81st session, beginning September 2026. This marks Bangladesh's return to this significant global role after nearly 40 years. India and Pakistan have extended congratulations. Dr. Rahman's leadership will shape discussions on international cooperation and global issues.

articles.title: New US hurdle for Indian students? University faces 2000-student risk
articles.description: A New Hampshire university could lose up to 2,000 international graduate students if the Department of Homeland Security fails to approve a doctoral programme application before July 1, lawmakers warn

articles.title: Some ‘sinned’ when they denied Ajit Pawar wanted merger of both NCPs: Supriya
articles.description: Some ‘sinned’ when they denied Ajit Pawar wanted merger of both NCPs: Supriya

articles.title: Fresh Israel-Hezbollah Clashes Erupt, Day After Trump's Halt-in-Hostilities Clai...
articles.description: Israeli military actions persisted in Lebanon despite claims from President Trump that both Israel and Hezbollah agreed to ceasefire commitments.

articles.title: Hezbollah's attacks keep coming, Bibi's top man keeps threatening Dahiyeh - who has the real plan?
articles.description: Israel’s Defence Minister Israel Katz declared that there is “no ceasefire” in Lebanon and warned that any continued attacks on Israeli settlements could trigger strikes on Beirut’s Dahiyeh district. His remarks came after reports that Israel had halted a planned assault on Beirut following diplomatic pressure and rising regional tensions. Katz said the IDF would continue operations against Hezbollah across Lebanon and insisted that Israel would maintain its security zone and military pressure until its objectives were achieved. He also claimed that hundreds of thousands of residents evacuated Dahiyeh after Israeli warnings, increasing pressure on Hezbollah and the Lebanese government. The comments come amid escalating cross-border attacks, reports of Hezbollah operations against Israeli targets, and growing concerns that the Israel-Lebanon conflict could reignite into a wider regional war involving Iran and other actors.

articles.title: Marco Rubio Says US Considering Naming Official To Run Ebola Response
articles.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio told a House of Representatives committee on Tuesday that the Trump administration is considering appointing a single official to coordinate its response to the Ebola outbreak in Africa.

articles.title: From Iran to another trap: Trump's US now prepares for two-front missile war it knows it’s losing?
articles.description: As tensions with China and Russia intensify, the U.S. Navy is investing $100 million to prepare for the missile threats it fears most. Using the supersonic GQM-163A Coyote target missile, American warships will train against sea-skimming attack profiles designed to replicate advanced Chinese and Russian anti-ship weapons, sharpening readiness for a potential future conflict in the Indo-Pacific and beyond.

articles.title: LIVE | 'WHAT ARE YOU HIDING': AG Blanche faces House hearing on Justice Dept oversight & funding
articles.description: Watch live as the U.S. House Appropriations subcommittee holds an oversight hearing on the Department of Justice with Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche as the witness.

articles.title: Iron dome not working at all? Hezbollah’s revenge leaves Israel trembling with non-stop attacks
articles.description: Hezbollah claimed it carried out 41 military operations within 24 hours, signaling continued pressure on Israeli positions amid ongoing tensions along the Lebanon-Israel frontier. According to statements attributed to the group, attacks targeted areas including the Galilee, Safed and Tiberias regions, while Israeli media reportedly recorded more than 70 rocket warning sirens across northern areas. Hezbollah also claimed strikes against Israeli armored vehicles and said guided missiles hit a Merkava tank. Meanwhile, senior Hezbollah figure Hassan Fadlallah reiterated demands for a comprehensive ceasefire covering land, air and sea, alongside an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon before broader negotiations can move forward.

articles.title: 'Russian drone, nothing else!': Ukraine UN envoy unleashes blistering rebuttal in UAV crash row
articles.description: Tensions flared at the United Nations after Ukraine’s ambassador forcefully rejected Russian claims surrounding the drone that crashed in Romania. Speaking during an emergency Security Council session requested by Bucharest, Ukrainian envoy Andriy Melnyk insisted the UAV was a Russian-made Geran-2 drone and dismissed any suggestion that Ukraine was responsible for the incident. Romania’s Foreign Minister Oana Toiu told the Security Council that Romanian investigators had conclusively identified the drone as Russian in origin after analysing recovered fragments, navigation systems and other components. The crash injured civilians and damaged a residential building in the Romanian city of Galati, marking one of the most serious spillover incidents affecting a NATO member since the start of the war.

articles.title: Indian man to be deported from New Zealand after being caught in underage sex ring, says he would face stigma back home
articles.description: Indian man to be deported from New Zealand after being caught in underage sex ring, says he would face stigma back home

articles.title: Amit Shah meets Nepal's RSP leader Rabi Lamichhane, vows to boost ties
articles.description: Union Home Minister Amit Shah and EAM S Jaishankar held meetings with a Nepalese delegation led by Rabi Lamichhane, Chairman of the Rastriya Swatantra Party, reaffirming their commitment to enhance the special ties between India and Nepal.
